automotive-ev-battery.title: The Big ICE Meltdown — May's China EV Sales Report
automotive-ev-battery.summary: China's electric vehicle market reached record penetration in May 2026, with plugin EVs capturing 63 percent market share as internal combustion engine sales crashed 39 percent. The surge reflects sustained EV demand growth and an influx of new models, signaling accelerating automotive industry transformation in the world's largest vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Is BYD Making A Better "Ferrari" Than Ferrari?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD's Denza Z sports car showcases the company's growing dominance in high-performance EVs, featuring a combined 1,180 kilowatts across three motors delivering 1,582 horsepower and acceleration from 0-100 km/h comparable to traditional supercars. The vehicle demonstrates how Chinese EV manufacturers are expanding beyond mass-market segments into premium performance categories.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Galp completes fast-charging corridor across Portugal
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Portuguese charging provider Galp has completed a comprehensive fast-charging corridor spanning the entire country from Porto in the north to the Algarve in the south along major A1 and A2 motorways. This infrastructure development addresses a critical need for EV owners traveling long distances across the Iberian Peninsula.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla's Level 2++ Supervised Full Self-Driving Approved In Belgium
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has obtained regulatory approval for its Supervised Full Self-Driving system in Belgium, expanding European deployment following prior approvals in the Netherlands, Lithuania, Estonia, and Denmark. The company must complete additional testing before rolling out the Level 2++ driver-assist technology to customers in the country.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Super-sized slot cars: these HD electric machines NEVER need plugged in
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A Kalmar Ottawa T2 EV electric terminal tractor has been successfully deployed on an in-road wireless charging system in Ljungby, Sweden, enabling continuous operations without requiring plug-in charging infrastructure. This breakthrough demonstrates the viability of dynamic charging technology for heavy-duty commercial vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: This electric car company just produced its one millionth electric motorcycle
automotive-ev-battery.summary: VinFast has achieved production of one million electric two-wheelers, establishing itself as a major force in the global e-mobility sector beyond its international electric vehicle ambitions. The Vietnamese company's dominance in electric motorcycles and scooters represents a significant but often overlooked segment of the broader EV industry.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Used tyres could make EVs quieter
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Researchers at Otto von Guericke University Magdeburg have developed a novel noise-reduction technology using recycled tire material to suppress unwanted humming sounds from EV power electronics. The innovation addresses a growing consumer concern as electric vehicles' inherent quietness sometimes creates perception issues with cabin noise.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric rally car revival continues with new, 280 hp Peugeot E-208 GTi
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Peugeot has unveiled the new E-208 GTi with 280 horsepower, debuting at the 24 Hours of LeMans and reviving the iconic hot hatch nameplate as an electric vehicle. The model joins other reborn heritage EV performance vehicles from manufacturers including Lancia and Renault, reflecting industry trends toward electrifying legendary automotive brand portfolios.

automotive-ev-battery.title: India: Meghalaya launches five electric buses in Shillong
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Meghalaya has deployed five electric buses in Shillong, marking the northeastern Indian state's inaugural adoption of electric public transportation infrastructure. The initiative represents growing electrification of India's urban transit systems as municipalities seek to reduce emissions and operational costs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Observations After Two Weeks with Tesla Hardware 4 & Full Self Driving V14 in My 2026 Tesla Model Y
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A long-term Tesla owner's experience with upgraded Hardware 4 and Full Self-Driving V14 in a new Model Y provides practical insights into the company's autonomous driving progression compared to earlier Hardware 3 systems. The firsthand assessment offers valuable perspective on Tesla's self-driving capabilities and hardware generational improvements for consumers considering adoption.

science-space.title: How Japanese scientists sent a real-life Transformer to the moon
science-space.summary: Japanese researchers successfully deployed an autonomous transforming rover to the lunar surface in 2024 that converted from a sphere into a functional robot. The innovative spacecraft demonstrated advanced robotics technology and expanded humanity's capabilities for lunar exploration.

science-space.title: Alien planet spins revealed a hidden clue to how worlds form
science-space.summary: Using the Keck Observatory, astronomers measured the rotational speeds of dozens of giant planets and brown dwarfs, finding that giant planets can spin faster than more massive brown dwarfs. The discovery suggests that magnetic fields and formation processes play a critical role in determining planetary rotation rates, challenging existing models of planetary formation.

science-space.title: Alaska's glaciers have a startling response to rising temperatures
science-space.summary: Researchers using radar satellites monitoring over 3,000 Alaskan glaciers found that each 1°C increase in summer temperature extends glacier melting by approximately three weeks. The study revealed that heat waves can strip up to 28% of protective snow cover, significantly accelerating ice loss and demonstrating the dramatic climate sensitivity of these glaciers.

science-space.title: Millions could see a rare sunset during the total solar eclipse on Aug. 12, 2026. Here's where to look
science-space.summary: A forthcoming total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026 will provide millions of observers with an extraordinary viewing opportunity of a rare sunset phenomenon. The article guides readers on optimal locations and timing for witnessing this significant astronomical event.

science-space.title: These tiny holes could change how the world cleans water
science-space.summary: Scientists have developed a nature-inspired membrane featuring perfectly uniform one-nanometer pores that filters molecules with exceptional precision. The breakthrough technology could revolutionize water treatment and separation processes across pharmaceuticals and textiles by reducing energy consumption and improving water reuse efficiency.

science-space.title: Scientists discover a surprising cancer link to Alzheimer's disease
science-space.summary: Researchers identified an unexpected connection between mutations associated with blood cancers and Alzheimer's disease, finding that these mutations may trigger excessive inflammation in the brain's immune cells. The discovery could enable development of new blood-based diagnostic methods and open therapeutic approaches adapted from cancer medicine.

science-space.title: A hidden gene finally explains this rare neurological disorder
science-space.summary: Scientists analyzing genetic data from nearly 3,000 patients identified mutations in the CD99L2 gene as a previously unknown cause of a rare movement disorder affecting coordination and muscle control. The finding reveals that this gene, previously thought to function only in immune system regulation, plays an essential role in maintaining nerve-cell communication.

science-space.title: Lucy's hunter revealed: Giant crocodile terrorized early human ancestors
science-space.summary: Paleontologists have identified a newly discovered giant crocodile species nicknamed "Lucy's hunter" that inhabited Ethiopian rivers over 3 million years ago alongside early human relatives. The apex predator likely posed a significant threat to hominins and dominated the ecosystem during this pivotal period in human evolution.

science-space.title: The missing notebooks that solved a 55-million-year-old fossil mystery
science-space.summary: Recovered field notebooks from the original collector have revealed the complete story of a spectacular 1.2-meter fossil fish discovered on a remote New Zealand cliff nearly three decades ago. The three-dimensional fossil belongs to an ancient tarpon-like predator that inhabited New Zealand waters approximately 55 million years ago.

science-space.title: Your brain can keep improving into your 90s, study finds
science-space.summary: A three-year study of nearly 4,000 adults aged 19 to 94 demonstrates that brain health can improve at any age through brief daily brain-training activities. The research challenges conventional wisdom about cognitive decline, showing measurable gains across thinking clarity, emotional well-being, and sense of purpose across all age groups.

world-news.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
world-news.summary: The United States and Iran appear close to finalizing the first stage of a peace deal, though the two nations differ on the signing timeline. Iran has stated an exact date has not been decided and the agreement will not be signed immediately, creating uncertainty around US President Trump's announcement.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump says deal to be signed today; Tehran urges caution
world-news.summary: The United States and Iran are negotiating the first stage of a comprehensive peace accord, with both sides signaling progress but disagreeing on the announcement and implementation timeline. The negotiations represent a significant diplomatic development in the ongoing US-Iran tensions.

world-news.title: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap population at 10 million
world-news.summary: Switzerland is preparing for a referendum on a population cap initiative proposed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, which frames it as a sustainability measure. Critics argue the proposal would create significant economic and social disruption if implemented.

world-news.title: Two killed in Israeli strike on Gaza
world-news.summary: An Israeli military strike on southern Gaza has resulted in two deaths and one injury, according to Palestinian health authorities. The incident reflects ongoing military operations in the region.

world-news.title: After battling armed attacks, Mauritania attempts to revive tourism
world-news.summary: Mauritania is working to restore its tourism sector after years of security challenges posed by armed groups affiliated with al-Qaeda, which began launching attacks in the mid-2000s. Improved security measures have contributed to a reduction in violence, enabling the country to pursue economic recovery.

world-news.title: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
world-news.summary: Major General Rabe Abubakar, a retired Nigerian military officer, has died while being held in kidnapping, highlighting persistent security challenges in parts of Nigeria. The incident underscores ongoing threats from armed groups in the country.

world-news.title: Why is the price of gold trending down?
world-news.summary: Gold prices have declined since the United States and Israel launched military operations against Iran in late February, creating uncertainty in global commodity markets. The geopolitical tensions are affecting precious metals valuations worldwide.

world-news.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
world-news.summary: Anthropic has suspended its newly released Claude Fable 5 artificial intelligence tool following concerns raised by the US government regarding cybersecurity vulnerabilities and hacking risks. The suspension reflects growing scrutiny of advanced AI systems and their potential national security implications.

world-news.title: America250 versus Freedom250: What to know about the US semiquincentennial
world-news.summary: The United States is preparing celebrations marking its 250th anniversary, though the observance has become contested amid questions about the incoming Trump administration's influence on the commemorative agenda. The debate reflects broader divisions over the country's historical narrative and future direction.

world-news.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
world-news.summary: Crews have begun work to remove former President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center following a court order, with the project commencing this weekend despite weather delays. The removal reflects ongoing legal and political disputes regarding the former president's legacy and public institutions.

finance-markets.title: AI is disrupting investment
finance-markets.summary: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping how investment firms allocate capital and manage portfolio diversification across all asset classes. The technology-driven shift is altering traditional investment strategies and risk management approaches globally.

finance-markets.title: AI is revolutionising the stock market
finance-markets.summary: The stock market is undergoing significant structural change as major technology companies transition from generating profits to requiring substantial capital investment. This shift raises questions about market stability if investor confidence deteriorates.

finance-markets.title: How to tame the inflation python
finance-markets.summary: Central banks risk repeating 2008 financial crisis mistakes by adopting a 'wait and see' monetary policy approach in controlling inflation. The article warns that hesitant policy responses could allow inflationary pressures to build dangerously.

finance-markets.title: CLO ETFs Boom on Higher Rates, Private Debt Woes
finance-markets.summary: Wall Street is promoting collateralized loan obligation exchange-traded funds as a vehicle for retail investors to capitalize on elevated interest rates while hedging against defaults in private credit markets. The product surge reflects growing concerns about private debt stability.

finance-markets.title: South Korea's World-Beating Stock Market Eyes Its MSCI Moment
finance-markets.summary: After experiencing significant volatility, South Korea's stock market is approaching a critical milestone with the potential for inclusion in MSCI's developed-market index. This development would represent a major recognition of the market's maturity and attract substantial international capital flows.

finance-markets.title: How Wall Street pulled off the biggest IPO in history for SpaceX
finance-markets.summary: Investment bankers successfully structured and marketed SpaceX's historic initial public offering by convincing investors to embrace speculative growth narratives despite substantial operating losses. The deal granted founder Elon Musk effective control of the publicly traded entity.

finance-markets.title: Investment firms join Donald Trump's $100bn race for Venezuelan oil
finance-markets.summary: Lionheart Capital and Keo Energy have established a Nasdaq-listed company to pursue Venezuelan oil assets as part of the Trump administration's broader Latin American energy strategy. The $100 billion initiative aims to capitalize on geopolitical shifts in the region.

finance-markets.title: Fed and BOE Stay Guarded After 100 Days of Iran War
finance-markets.summary: Global central banks including the Federal Reserve and Bank of England are maintaining cautious stances as geopolitical tensions persist, uncertain whether the Iran conflict poses greater near-term risks to inflation or economic growth. Policy decisions in the coming week will reflect their assessment of these competing pressures.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic suspends latest AI models after US blocks access to foreigners
finance-markets.summary: The Trump administration has directed AI company Anthropic to restrict foreign national access to its latest models on national security grounds, forcing the firm to suspend certain product releases. This marks a significant government intervention in the AI sector's global distribution strategy.

finance-markets.title: Trump says US and Iran will sign deal on Sunday to reopen Strait
finance-markets.summary: The Trump administration announced a deal to reopen the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz would be signed on Sunday, though Iran has cautioned that timing remains uncertain. Pakistan indicated the interim agreement extending the ceasefire could be finalized within 24 hours, with broader negotiations potentially following.

tech-ai.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House's Anthropic Fable ban
tech-ai.summary: According to the Wall Street Journal, Amazon's cybersecurity research and conversations between CEO Andy Jassy and the White House triggered an export control directive that led Anthropic to cut off access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The research reportedly demonstrated that the models could be manipulated through specific prompts to provide information usable in cyberattacks, raising national security concerns.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
tech-ai.summary: The U.S. government ordered Anthropic to block all access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for users worldwide, including employees, due to national security concerns. Anthropic complied with the order but expressed disagreement, stating the government did not provide specific details about its security concerns and arguing that a narrow potential vulnerability should not justify recalling models deployed to hundreds of millions of users.

tech-ai.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown
tech-ai.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been instrumental in triggering government action against Anthropic's models, having raised cybersecurity concerns with federal officials before the company cut off access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The situation suggests potential corporate involvement in shaping government AI policy decisions.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic's safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic's public disclosure of a potential security vulnerability in Fable 5 has backfired, with the government ordering immediate access restrictions rather than allowing the company to address the issue through standard procedures. The incident highlights tensions between AI safety transparency and government regulatory response, with Anthropic expressing strong disagreement with the government's decision.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
tech-ai.summary: Multiple state attorneys general have launched investigations into OpenAI's practices, covering concerns ranging from advertising policies to the handling of health data. The investigation represents another regulatory challenge for the leading AI company amid growing government scrutiny of the AI industry.

tech-ai.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing's demand
tech-ai.summary: Meta is reportedly dismantling its $2 billion acquisition of Manus following a directive from Chinese authorities to reverse the transaction. The move represents a significant setback for Meta's hardware and VR ambitions in what appears to be a geopolitical challenge to the tech giant's international expansion.

tech-ai.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
tech-ai.summary: KPMG withdrew a report on AI usage after discovering the document contained AI-generated hallucinations and inaccurate information. The incident underscores ongoing reliability concerns about AI systems even when applied to analysis of AI itself.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
tech-ai.summary: TechCrunch provides comprehensive coverage of SpaceX's anticipated initial public offering, including analysis of potential beneficiaries, pre-IPO investment opportunities, and details from the company's regulatory filings. The coverage tracks SpaceX's evolution from startup to potential publicly-traded company.

tech-ai.title: The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world cyberattacks
tech-ai.summary: The FBI has constructed a simulated small town within a facility in Alabama to serve as a dedicated training ground for cybersecurity professionals to practice responding to real-world cyberattack scenarios. The facility represents the agency's effort to better prepare defenders for emerging cyber threats.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft hasn't ruled out spinning off Xbox
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is preparing significant layoffs in its Xbox division and reportedly considering dramatic restructuring options, including the potential spinoff of Xbox as a separate company. The company is also reevaluating plans for its next-generation Project Helix console as it seeks to make the gaming unit more financially sustainable.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Semiconductor Startup Funding Still Running Hot
startups-vc.summary: The semiconductor startup sector continues to attract significant venture capital investment, with approximately $10 billion deployed into seed through pre-IPO rounds in 2026 alone. This sustained funding momentum reflects robust investor confidence in the chip startup space despite broader market volatility.

startups-vc.title: Shopify Turns 20! 5 Interesting Learnings from Shopify at $13B+ in ARR. And Accelerating
startups-vc.summary: Shopify has reached a $13 billion+ annual recurring revenue run rate while celebrating its 20th anniversary, processing over $100 billion in merchant sales quarterly. The company defies the typical trajectory of legacy software platforms by continuing to accelerate rather than decline on its installed base.

startups-vc.title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
startups-vc.summary: BillionToOne has become Y Combinator's fourth publicly traded biotech company following its initial public offering. The company has established itself as a leader in genetic testing, with one in eleven babies born in the United States now tested using its fetal genetic test.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies experienced layoffs in 2025, with mass job cuts continuing into 2026. The tracker documents ongoing workforce reductions across the technology sector.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho, a Y Combinator S16 alumnus, has completed its initial public offering after building one of India's largest e-commerce platforms. The company has empowered millions of small merchants to grow their businesses through its platform.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, has gone public and become Y Combinator's first company to list in India. The platform serves tens of millions of users seeking simplified access to stocks, mutual funds, and ETFs.

startups-vc.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
startups-vc.summary: Andrew Yang has identified a significant startup opportunity in reducing the cost of living for Americans across categories including housing, food, and wireless services. Yang's focus reflects growing entrepreneurial interest in consumer cost reduction as a business model.

startups-vc.title: Lovable, Harvey & Assembly AI: How the Fastest AI Companies Rebuilt Customer Success. And Why NPS and Activity Scores Are Dead
startups-vc.summary: Fast-growing AI companies are fundamentally restructuring customer success practices in response to changing market dynamics, with industry leaders arguing that traditional metrics like NPS and activity scores are becoming obsolete. This shift reflects broader changes in how AI-driven businesses engage and retain customers.

startups-vc.title: Why It's So Hard for Older B2B Leaders to Compete in AI: Your Customers Can Do A Lot in Claude for $20-$200/Month. And You're Paying $1.00 Per API Call For the Good Stuff.
startups-vc.summary: Established B2B companies face competitive challenges in the AI market as customers gain access to powerful AI capabilities at low consumer price points, creating pricing and value proposition pressures for legacy software vendors. Cost control strategies that prioritize minimal API spending may actually undermine competitive positioning in the AI era.

startups-vc.title: Who Got the Most Leads at SaaStr AI Annual 2026? The Top 15 Tell You Exactly Where B2B Budget Is Going
startups-vc.summary: The SaaStr AI 2026 conference sponsor leaderboard reveals where B2B and AI industry investment and attention are concentrated, with Replit leading sponsor engagement metrics by generating 1,423 qualified leads from over 10,000 attendees. The data provides insight into which companies and solutions are capturing buyer attention in the evolving B2B AI market.

health-biotech.title: FDA approves Sanofi diabetes drug for children with stage 3 diabetes
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has approved Sanofi's diabetes drug for children with stage 3 disease, resolving a prior dispute between career staff and political appointees at the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research. The approval marks a significant regulatory decision in pediatric diabetes treatment options.

health-biotech.title: Takeda's $4B TYK2 drug tops Bristol Myers' Sotyktu in head-to-head test
health-biotech.summary: Takeda's zasocitinib has demonstrated superiority over Bristol Myers Squibb's Sotyktu in a head-to-head clinical trial, potentially giving Takeda a competitive advantage in the immunology market. The results validate Takeda's long-standing confidence in the drug's efficacy profile.

health-biotech.title: Novartis RNA drug acquired in $12B Avidity deal notches a trial win
health-biotech.summary: Del-brax, an antisense oligonucleotide conjugate acquired by Novartis in its $12 billion Avidity Biologics deal, has met its primary endpoint in a Phase 1/2 trial for muscle-wasting disease. The positive data validate the therapeutic approach and Novartis's significant acquisition investment.

health-biotech.title: WHO director-general is profoundly concerned after visit to Ebola outbreak area
health-biotech.summary: The World Health Organization's director-general has expressed profound concern following an on-site visit to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The statement underscores the severity of the current outbreak situation and WHO's engagement with the crisis.

health-biotech.title: J&J looks to widen Imaavy's use; $300M backs rare disease drug launch
health-biotech.summary: Johnson & Johnson is pursuing expanded indications for Imaavy based on new data suggesting effectiveness in treating autoimmune anemia, while a separate $300 million funding round supports the launch of a rare disease drug. The developments indicate broadening therapeutic applications and continued investment in niche disease markets.

health-biotech.title: Trump administration revisits policy to close Medicare drug price negotiation loophole
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration is revisiting policy measures to plug a loophole in Medicare's drug price negotiation framework. The initiative addresses ongoing concerns about pricing mechanisms within the federal healthcare system.

health-biotech.title: WuXi AppTec sues Pentagon in challenge over inclusion on Chinese military list
health-biotech.summary: WuXi AppTec has filed a legal challenge against the Department of Defense contesting its inclusion on a list of companies associated with the Chinese military. The lawsuit represents a significant dispute over national security designations affecting the contract research organization sector.

health-biotech.title: Rapport 'fully prepared' to launch seizure drug solo, CEO says
health-biotech.summary: Rapport Therapeutics' CEO has stated the company is prepared to independently commercialize its seizure drug, having assembled a robust investor syndicate. The company is positioning itself to join the ranks of successful commercial-stage neuroscience biotech firms.

health-biotech.title: Summit pulls $500M share sale a day after announcing it
health-biotech.summary: Summit Therapeutics has withdrawn a $500 million secondary offering announced the previous day, citing deteriorating market conditions. The cancellation follows the developer of a PD-1/VEGF cancer drug struggling to generate positive stock momentum after recent data announcements.

health-biotech.title: Sensorion, citing Regeneron competition, shifts focus to a different hearing loss therapy
health-biotech.summary: Sensorion has halted development of its gene therapy for OTOF-related hearing loss following a strategic review that identified heightened competition from Regeneron. The company is redirecting resources toward alternative hearing loss treatment candidates.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto's next billion-dollar hacker may move at superhuman speed
crypto-web3.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 AI model presents significant cybersecurity risks to the cryptocurrency industry, with potential vulnerabilities that could enable sophisticated attacks on DeFi platforms. The DeFi sector, which has already suffered over $840 million in losses to hacks this year, faces particular exposure if the AI model's safety mechanisms are compromised.

crypto-web3.title: US Government Orders Anthropic to Pull Claude Fable, Mythos AI Models
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. government has issued orders requiring Anthropic to withdraw its Claude Fable and Mythos AI models, citing security vulnerabilities. Anthropic has contested the directive, asserting that the identified vulnerability is already prevalent across the broader AI industry.

crypto-web3.title: Here's what SpaceX's IPO means for its $1.3 billion bitcoin reserve
crypto-web3.summary: SpaceX's initial public offering marks a significant milestone for cryptocurrency adoption, as the company becomes one of the largest publicly traded entities to hold bitcoin as a treasury reserve. The company's earnings performance will provide crucial insights into whether institutional bitcoin holdings prove resilient during bear market conditions.

crypto-web3.title: Google Sues Chinese Crime Group for Allegedly Using Gemini AI for Mass Phishing Scams
crypto-web3.summary: Google has filed legal action against a Chinese criminal network accused of weaponizing its Gemini AI model to perpetrate large-scale phishing operations that compromised millions of credit card numbers and specifically targeted cryptocurrency investors. The case highlights emerging security threats at the intersection of AI technology and financial crime.

crypto-web3.title: Saylor to Musk: Thanks to you, 25% of 'Mag8' firms now hold bitcoin
crypto-web3.summary: Michael Saylor highlighted that one-quarter of the Magnificent Eight mega-cap technology firms now hold bitcoin on their balance sheets, with SpaceX's recent IPO being cited as a catalyst for this institutional adoption trend. The milestone reflects growing mainstream acceptance of cryptocurrency as a corporate treasury asset among the world's largest companies.

crypto-web3.title: Bybit, Binance, Bitget cancel tokenized SpaceX IPO allocations after share shortage
crypto-web3.summary: Three major cryptocurrency exchanges have cancelled tokenized allocations for SpaceX's IPO due to insufficient share availability and are issuing full refunds with additional compensation to affected users. The incident underscores operational challenges in delivering tokenized securities for high-demand offerings.

crypto-web3.title: Wall Street is moving past crypto pilots and deeper into Ethereum, says Etherealize founder
crypto-web3.summary: Etherealize's cofounder Vivek Raman indicated that traditional financial institutions are transitioning beyond experimental cryptocurrency projects to substantive Ethereum integration, with the infrastructure largely established but adoption not yet reflected in ETH valuations. The shift signals growing institutional confidence in the Ethereum ecosystem's maturity.

crypto-web3.title: Perpetual futures could become crypto's next ETF moment
crypto-web3.summary: Kraken's head of derivatives John Palmer predicts that newly approved U.S. perpetual futures contracts will drive cryptocurrency adoption among sophisticated traders first, followed by broader institutional participation. The development mirrors the transformative impact of bitcoin ETF products on mainstream cryptocurrency investment.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase quantum report flags exchange cold wallets among millions of bitcoin exposed by address reuse
crypto-web3.summary: A Coinbase quantum computing security report has identified millions of bitcoin at risk due to address reuse vulnerabilities, including holdings in exchange cold wallets, and proposes potential mitigation strategies such as migration deadlines and asset freezing. The findings underscore ongoing cybersecurity challenges related to legacy address management practices.

crypto-web3.title: Tokenization mirrors the $20 trillion ETF boom as blockchain and AI converge, Ondo exec says
crypto-web3.summary: Ondo's new head of portfolio products John Hoffman noted that tokenization is establishing infrastructure for autonomous investing and real-time portfolio management, positioning blockchain as analogous to the transformative $20 trillion ETF industry. The convergence of blockchain and artificial intelligence is expected to enable significant advances in investment automation.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes across U.S. schools and universities. The attackers threatened to leak sensitive data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions unless a ransom was paid.

cybersecurity.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
cybersecurity.summary: Splunk released security updates addressing a critical vulnerability in Splunk Enterprise that enables unauthenticated attackers to execute remote code and perform arbitrary file operations. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20253 with a CVSS score of 9.8, affects versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts including those of the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were compromised after attackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. Instructions for the exploit circulated on Telegram, resulting in accounts being briefly defaced with pro-Iranian content.

cybersecurity.title: Chinese Hackers Hijack Auth Flow, Spy on Isolated Network for a Decade
cybersecurity.summary: Chinese state-sponsored hackers maintained a decade-long presence within a target organization's authentication infrastructure, providing them with complete visibility into administrative activities. The attackers achieved unprecedented persistence through control of the authentication stack on what was designed to be an isolated network.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware gang has emerged as the second most active ransomware operation by victim count, leveraging an aggressive recruitment strategy offering affiliates 90 percent of ransom proceeds. Krebs on Security examines available evidence to identify the real-world administrator behind the rapidly growing criminal enterprise.

cybersecurity.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have identified a new attack class called Agentjacking that manipulates artificial intelligence coding agents into executing arbitrary code on developer machines. The attack exploits the Sentry error-tracking platform by injecting crafted error reports that trick AI agents into running malicious code.

cybersecurity.title: phpBB Forum Fixes Auth Bypass Bug Lurking for a Decade
cybersecurity.summary: phpBB forum software has patched a critical authentication bypass vulnerability that existed undetected for approximately ten years. The flaw allowed attackers to authenticate as any user, including administrators, potentially compromising millions of forum installations worldwide.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released near-record volumes of security patches this month, with AI tools proving effective at identifying vulnerabilities in human-coded software. The findings demonstrate that artificial intelligence platforms, while susceptible to social engineering, excel at discovering security flaws in traditional code.

cybersecurity.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
cybersecurity.summary: Following a U.S. government order citing national security concerns, Anthropic announced it will disable access to its advanced Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models for foreign nationals globally. Anthropic is complying with the directive while disputing the government's characterization of the security threat underlying the order.

cybersecurity.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
cybersecurity.summary: State-sponsored cybercrime groups from North Korea and China are expanding their targeting of financial institutions and other businesses across the Asia-Pacific region, with evidence suggesting North Korea's economic growth is partly attributable to proceeds from these coordinated cyber operations. The threat actors continue to refine tactics based on their regional successes.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: How a record-high 'energy imbalance' is driving global warming
energy-climate.summary: New research demonstrates that Earth's energy imbalance has reached record levels, accelerating global warming at unprecedented rates. The analysis examines how decades of greenhouse gas emissions are creating an increasingly severe imbalance between incoming and outgoing radiation.

energy-climate.title: Solar capacity up 20% from last summer: EIA
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. Energy Information Administration projects that utility-scale solar generation will increase 19% this summer compared to the previous year, reflecting a substantial 20% growth in overall solar capacity. This expansion underscores the rapid acceleration of solar energy adoption across the country.

energy-climate.title: 1M+ customers have connected solar to PG&E's grid
energy-climate.summary: Pacific Gas & Electric announced that over one million customers have connected distributed solar systems to its grid, marking a significant milestone in the transition toward interactive energy systems. The achievement reflects an industry-wide shift from passive grid models to customer-centered renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: Prysmian completes cable installation for Sofia offshore wind farm
energy-climate.summary: Prysmian Group has finished its cable installation contract for RWE's 1.4-gigawatt Sofia offshore wind farm located on Dogger Bank in the North Sea. The project represents a major step forward in expanding European offshore wind capacity.

energy-climate.title: Cypress Creek secures $3.5bn for Steel River Energy project
energy-climate.summary: Cypress Creek Energy has secured $3.5 billion in financing for the first two phases of the Steel River Energy Center in the United States. The substantial investment underscores growing capital commitments to large-scale renewable energy infrastructure development.

energy-climate.title: Rolls-Royce to build four BESS facilities for Sunly in Latvia
energy-climate.summary: Rolls-Royce Power Systems and Sunly have finalized contracts for constructing four large-scale battery energy storage system facilities across Latvia. The project addresses growing European demand for grid-scale storage solutions to support renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: Meta expands US solar portfolio, inks PPA with Zelestra
energy-climate.summary: Meta has executed a new power purchase agreement with renewable energy company Zelestra to support additional solar projects across the United States. The expansion reflects corporate commitments to securing renewable energy to power data center operations.

energy-climate.title: Port of Valencia deploying PV on breakwaters
energy-climate.summary: The Port of Valencia is installing solar panels on breakwaters through an EU-funded initiative to advance renewable energy adoption at Mediterranean ports. The pilot project aims to validate the technical, economic, and environmental viability of large-scale vertical photovoltaic installations.

energy-climate.title: First 9-in-1 heat pump transforms load into an energy hub
energy-climate.summary: Differ Power and a venture led by Tesla veteran Drew Baglino have developed an advanced heat pump system that integrates AI data center cooling with automotive-grade thermal technology to serve as a home's central energy management hub. The innovation combines multiple thermal functions into a single integrated platform.

energy-climate.title: What could save Arizona tens of millions in annual customer and infrastructure costs? Residential pool pumps.
energy-climate.summary: Arizona State University researchers found that shifting residential pool pump operations from nighttime to midday could displace approximately 820 megawatts of demand into off-peak periods, generating substantial cost savings for utilities and customers. The demand-shifting strategy demonstrates the potential of distributed residential loads in grid optimization.

asia-pacific.title: Will turmoil around former leader Ma Ying-jeou affect Taiwan’s local elections?
asia-pacific.summary:
What began as an internal personnel dispute at the Ma Ying-jeou Foundation has evolved into an increasingly bitter and publicly fought battle that is engulfing the former Taiwanese leader whose political legacy it is meant to preserve.
Allegations of financial misconduct at Ma’s foundation have spiralled into criminal complaints, publicly traded accusations among long-time allies and an unprecedented intervention by his family.
The dispute has also raised broader questions about Ma’s health,...

asia-pacific.title: What does North Korea get from its blossoming ties with Russia?
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korean leader Kim Jong-un’s latest message of reassurance to Russian President Vladimir Putin, coming shortly after Chinese President Xi Jinping’s visit to Pyongyang, constitutes a form of strategic hedging, according to observers.
The letter suggested that Pyongyang-Moscow ties were evolving beyond a largely transactional relationship into a firm military alliance, even as North Korea sought to rekindle its traditional “blood alliance” with China, they said.
In a congratulatory message...

asia-pacific.title: Why executive branches are best placed to gauge national security risks
asia-pacific.summary:
The recent enactment of a subsidiary legislation in Hong Kong regarding the appropriate procedure to be followed in cases of non-national security offences involving national security considerations not surprisingly spurred some to revisit the chief executive’s power, granted under the national security law in 2020, to issue a certificate as to whether an act involves issues of national security.
That some people express concern that such an important task is left to the executive and not the...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Not giving up on any market’: John Lee on his strategy to push Hong Kong’s interests
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong is determined to expand into new markets while consolidating ties with traditional partners and “will not give up any markets” despite geopolitical pressures, the city leader has said.
In an interview with the South China Morning Post reviewing his past four years in office, Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu said the United States remained an important market for Hong Kong despite volatile relations between Beijing and Washington, while also stressing the interactions would mostly be...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man weighing 63kg believes online products could help gain weight loses 6.5kg
asia-pacific.summary:
A Shanghai man who wanted to gain weight spent 10,000 yuan (US$1,500) on online health products he thought would do the job only to end up losing 6.5kg in a month.
The man, surnamed Liu, has trended on mainland social media after his experience was reported by the Shanghai Morning Post.
Liu, a truck driver, who is 1.78m tall and weighs 53kg, always thought he was too thin.
In March, he saw the social media account of an influencer surnamed Chen who shared her knowledge about helping people put..

asia-pacific.title: Japan adds Indonesia to ‘network of navies’ after Australia, Philippines
asia-pacific.summary:
Indonesia sits at the confluence of the world’s busiest sea lanes. Its coastline stretches nearly 55,000km (34,000 miles) and its waters encompass the Malacca and Lombok straits, chokepoints through which trillions of US dollars in global trade pass annually.
Yet for years the nation’s navy has lacked the subsurface awareness to monitor, let alone counter, what moves beneath the waves.
Japan intends to change that. Tokyo confirmed on June 5 that the two countries had agreed to begin formal talks

asia-pacific.title: China’s Geely Auto to slash excess capacity amid overhaul to boost carmaker’s global edge
asia-pacific.summary:
Geely Auto, which is locked in a fierce battle for dominance against BYD in China’s crowded automotive market, has pledged to purge excess capacity through an asset restructuring while ramping up its go-global drive with an eye on greater international competitiveness.
Chairman Li Shufu said during the Chongqing Auto Show on Friday that the Hong Kong-listed carmaker would assess the oversupply of capacity across all units to determine whether to close, suspend, merge or sell redundant production

asia-pacific.title: How did a Venezuelan dropout become a crime boss on the US hit list?
asia-pacific.summary:
The Tren de Aragua leader killed in a US-Venezuelan raid was a high school dropout who lived in comfort behind bars as he transformed a prison gang into one of the most powerful and extensive criminal organisations in Latin America.
Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, alias Nino Guerrero, or “child warrior”, died at age 42 in a raid announced Friday by President Donald Trump and later confirmed by Venezuela.
Founded in Venezuela in 2014, Tren de Aragua has been designated a terrorist...

asia-pacific.title: Trump to name James McDonald – one of his lawyers – as Wall Street’s top cop
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he planned to appoint James McDonald as US Attorney for the Southern District of New York, the federal prosecutor whose office handles many of the most high-profile cases involving Wall Street.
McDonald would replace Jay Clayton, whom Trump nominated as US director of national intelligence less than two weeks after a congressional backlash against his interim appointee, housing official Bill Pulte.
Trump had tapped Pulte after Tulsi Gabbard, the...

asia-pacific.title: Dear You – the Chinese migration film igniting a propaganda debate in Southeast Asia
asia-pacific.summary:
In less than two months, the indie film Dear You has become an unlikely mass hit.
Shot in the Teochew language from the Chaoshan region of southern China’s Guangdong province, the movie follows a man uncovering his family’s past by tracing remittance letters sent home from Thailand.
As he pieces together his grandfather’s life story, the movie also explores Chinese migration to the region and the diaspora’s struggle to preserve its heritage.
In one scene, an elderly schoolmaster risks arrest in.
